assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends STABLE release channel with comment 'More predictable than RAPID, more secure than REGULAR'

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: Response shows maintenance exclusions with scope: NO_UPGRADES and provides bash script examples for adding maintenance exclusions during training periods

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: Response sets maintenance window to 02:00 daily and includes bash script showing weekly Sunday maintenance window configuration

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: Response clearly separates systemNodePool for infrastructure and h100NodePools for training, with different taints and labels to ensure isolation

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: Response explicitly sets autoUpgrade: false for H100 training node pools and includes planned node pool rotation strategy for manual upgrades

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: Response includes detailed checkpointing configuration with CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL environment variable, persistent volume claims for checkpoints, and automated backup CronJob

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GPU driver version testing or staging validation requirements

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: Response mentions ADVANCED_DATAPATH for better GPU networking but does not specifically address GPUDirect/RDMA networking version compatibility requirements
